title: pilosella densiflora subsp. psammotropha
intro: Pilosella densiflora subsp. psammotropha, also known as sandwort or mountain sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Pilosella densiflora subsp. psammotropha has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Pilosella densiflora subsp. psammotropha is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
